title: polypodium zeylanicum
intro: Polypodium zeylanicum, also known as Indian fern or Sri Lankan polypody, is a type of flowering plant belonging to the Polypodiaceae family, commonly found in the tropical forests of Sri Lanka and other parts of Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, derived from its leaves and rhizomes. The health benefits of Polypodium zeylanicum include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. In traditional herbal medicine, preparations of Polypodium zeylanicum include teas, infusions, and decoctions made from the plant's leaves and rhizomes, although its use should be consulted with a healthcare professional.
